top_signals.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Warns Inflation Expectations Are Rising
top_signals.summary: South Africa’s central bank governor said policymakers are seeing early signs of second-round inflation effects as underlying price pressures build, emphasizing the need to act.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Massive bonuses for South Korea's chip workers puts central bank on inflation alert
top_signals.summary: Workers from tech industries receive bonus worth millions of won, prompting the Bank of Korea to warn of the upward pressure to inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: AI is making crypto security cheaper, faster and harder to ignore
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
top_signals.summary: Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin's future as revolutionary as the smartphone, according to CoinDesk
top_signals.summary: CoinDesk’s president of indices and data has a message for investors: Don’t count out bitcoin.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: The U.S. States Leading the Backlash Against Data Centers
top_signals.summary: Several U.S. states are concerned about the rapid expansion of data centres, as consumers pressure legislators to address rising utility bills and other energy concerns. Data centres are expected to be a major consumer of U.S. power by 2030 and beyond, as several tech companies expand their network, which is growing consumer concerns around the country’s power supply and demand. This has led several states to halt or even ban data centre development. Globally, data centres are expected to consume twice as much power and water by 2030, according…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
